us.label: US News
us.subtitle: Top domestic stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: AP-NORC poll finds US support for Israel weakening among Democrats
bullets: About one-third of US adults said Israel has committed genocide in Gaza.
Roughly 58% of Democrats said the US is too supportive of Israel.
The poll was conducted June 11–17 among 3,040 adults.

articles.title: Trump warns Iran of renewed US action if nuclear deal fails
bullets: Trump said the US would either reach a deal with Iran or “finish the job.”
Indirect US-Iran talks ended last week without public signs of progress.
Iranian officials rejected Trump’s threat and called for respectful dialogue.

articles.title: Ford recalls more than 110,000 US Mustangs over wiper and drivetrain issues
bullets: NHTSA said Ford is recalling 110,626 US vehicles in two separate actions.
One recall covers Mustang and Mustang GTD wiper and washer problems.
Another covers Mustang Mach-E vehicles with a possible rear pinion shaft fracture.

articles.title: United Airlines must face lawsuit over window seats without windows
bullets: A federal judge rejected United’s bid to dismiss the proposed class action.
Passengers say they paid extra for window seats that had no window views.
United said it added more detail to its seat-selection process.

articles.title: Toyota plans $3.6 billion Texas plant and shifts some Tacoma output from Mexico
bullets: Toyota said it will build a new plant on its San Antonio manufacturing campus.
The facility is expected to open by 2030 and create about 2,000 jobs.
Toyota will shift some Tacoma pickup production from Baja California to Texas.

global.label: Global
global.subtitle: Major worldwide developments from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: WHO says Congo Ebola outbreak remains in expansion phase
bullets: WHO said the outbreak has not stabilized and is still expanding.
Congo has confirmed 1,561 cases and 506 deaths.
The outbreak involves the Bundibugyo species of Ebola.

articles.title: Nine Pakistani policemen killed in Balochistan militant clash
bullets: Authorities said nine policemen and 15 militants were killed overnight.
The Pakistani Taliban claimed responsibility for the attack in Ziarat district.
Five officers were still missing after the clash.

articles.title: NATO allies discuss Hormuz tensions with Gulf Arab ministers
bullets: NATO ministers met Gulf Arab counterparts on the sidelines of the Ankara summit.
A Franco-British maritime mission proposal was discussed.
UKMTO reported a tanker was struck east of Oman.

articles.title: Tornadoes hit central China as Typhoon Bavi approaches offshore
bullets: Severe storms damaged parts of central China.
Authorities were monitoring Typhoon Bavi offshore.
The weather system added pressure after days of hazardous conditions.

articles.title: China and Japan trade accounts of Senkaku Islands confrontation
bullets: Beijing and Tokyo issued conflicting accounts of an encounter near the islands.
The disputed islands are known as Senkaku in Japan and Diaoyu in China.
The episode added to tensions in the East China Sea.

india.label: India News
india.subtitle: Top India-focused stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: India to supply BrahMos and Astra missiles to Indonesia
bullets: An Indian government official said India will supply BrahMos and Astra systems.
The announcement came during Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s Indonesia visit.
The deal adds a major defence element to India-Indonesia ties.

articles.title: Modi meets Indonesia’s Prabowo for defence and food-security talks
bullets: Modi was scheduled to meet President Prabowo Subianto in Jakarta.
Officials said talks included defence, health, space, pharmaceuticals and food security.
Modi’s trip also includes Australia and New Zealand.

articles.title: India’s new transgender rules disrupt access to care, doctors say
bullets: Reuters reported clinics have paused or delayed some gender-affirming care.
The March law narrowed eligibility for legal gender recognition.
Activists and doctors warned the rules create uncertainty for patients and providers.

articles.title: Search continues for six Andhra fishermen after boat capsizes
bullets: One fisherman swam for about two hours and reached a merchant vessel.
The Navy airlifted him to Visakhapatnam for medical observation.
Coast Guard and Navy teams continued searching for six missing crew members.

articles.title: Clearance sought for Indira Point lighthouse protection and tourism facilities
bullets: Officials sought approvals for work at India’s southernmost point on Great Nicobar.
Plans include lighthouse protection, a convention centre and a museum.
The proposal falls within ecologically sensitive coastal regulation zones.

stocks.label: US Stocks
stocks.subtitle: Key US market movers and trading stories

articles.title: S&P 500 and Nasdaq rise sharply as Broadcom leads chip rally
bullets: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq ended sharply higher Monday.
Broadcom jumped 3.7% after extending its Apple chip deal.
The Philadelphia semiconductor index rose 2.2% after two losing sessions.

articles.title: Nasdaq futures fall as Samsung results fail to calm AI-chip concerns
bullets: Nasdaq futures dropped Tuesday as chip shares weakened.
Micron, Western Digital and Sandisk fell in premarket trading.
SpaceX shares also slipped ahead of their Nasdaq-100 debut.

articles.title: Rivian shares fall after 75-million-share offering plan
bullets: Rivian slipped in extended trading after announcing a public share offering.
The company also forecast second-quarter revenue above analyst estimates.
The offering raised investor concerns about dilution.

articles.title: Fiserv rises after talks to sell debit network to major US banks
bullets: Fiserv held talks with banks including JPMorgan and Bank of America.
The talks involve its STAR debit and ATM network business.
Fiserv shares rose in after-hours trading after the report.

articles.title: Crinetics doubles after Vertex agrees to $10 billion takeover
bullets: Vertex offered $85 per Crinetics share in cash.
The price represents a 102% premium to Crinetics’ Monday close.
Vertex shares slipped after hours while Crinetics doubled.

articles.title: Toyota announces $3.6 billion Texas expansion
bullets: Toyota will build a new 2.5-million-square-foot facility in San Antonio.
The company expects the plant to open by 2030.
Some Tacoma production will move from Mexico to Texas.

articles.title: Vertex to buy Crinetics for $10 billion in rare-disease expansion
bullets: The deal adds rare hormonal-disease treatments to Vertex’s portfolio.
Crinetics shareholders will receive $85 per share in cash.
The companies said the assets could exceed $5 billion in peak annual sales.

articles.title: Walmart cuts barbecue staple prices after Trump says retailer acted at his request
bullets: Walmart said it would reduce prices on meat, chips and soda.
Trump said Walmart acted at his administration’s request.
Walmart cited a drop in a one-pound 73% ground beef roll price.

articles.title: Fiserv explores sale of debit-card network to big US banks
bullets: Talks have included JPMorgan, Bank of America, Wells Fargo and PNC.
The STAR Network handles debit, ATM and e-commerce routing.
Reuters reported no deal is certain and discussions could fall apart.

articles.title: United faces lawsuit over paid window seats that lacked windows
bullets: A judge allowed passengers’ breach claims to proceed.
The lawsuits seek damages for more than 1 million passengers per carrier.
Delta is facing a related case in federal court in Brooklyn.

articles.title: Samsung flags 19-fold profit jump, but shares slump on AI-boom doubts
bullets: Samsung estimated second-quarter operating profit at 89.4 trillion won.
The figure beat LSEG SmartEstimate expectations.
Samsung shares fell as investors questioned AI-chip demand durability.

articles.title: Meta says four US states seek $1.4 trillion in youth-safety penalties
bullets: Meta disclosed the figure in a court filing ahead of an August trial.
The states allege Facebook and Instagram were designed to addict young users.
Meta’s filing said the requested penalties approach its market capitalization.

articles.title: Google and RWE back Proxima Fusion’s €411 million financing round
bullets: Proxima Fusion raised €411 million from investors including Google and RWE.
The round valued the Munich-based startup at €2.4 billion.
RWE will partner on a stellarator fusion plant site in Bavaria.

articles.title: Synopsys shifts away from chip-fab control software toward AI design
bullets: Sources said Synopsys plans to stop offering some manufacturing control software.
The affected products are used by major semiconductor makers.
Synopsys said it is focusing resources on higher-value products.

articles.title: Broadcom shares rise after Apple silicon partnership extended through 2031
bullets: Broadcom and Apple expanded a long-running custom silicon collaboration.
Broadcom shares closed 3.7% higher Monday after the announcement.
The deal covers custom ASIC silicon products for future Apple devices.
